📦
Goodera Procurement & Logistics Model
GTM Supply Chain Planning Tool · 2026 Program Year
🗺️ What this model does
📊
Historical Analysis
Analyses 1,285+ events across 100+ US cities. Maps kit distribution, revenue, and procurement costs per kit type and source.
🔮
Forecast & Selection
Apply a multiplier to project 2026 volumes. Use the rule builder to select kit types for supply chain analysis.
⚖️
Scenario Comparison
Compares 4 scenarios — BAU direct delivery, bulk procurement, via 1 warehouse, or via 2 warehouses — on cost, savings, and margin.
🚀 How to use · Step by step
1
Upload your event data
Use the data uploader below to load your events CSV/JSON, or proceed with the demo data already loaded.
2
Set your assumptions in ⚙️ Inputs
Freight rates (sea/air ₹/VKG), warehouse locations and ops costs, procurement tiers, forecast multiplier.
3
Select kits in 🔮 Forecast
Use the rule builder — e.g. "India kits top 80% volume" + "keyword: robot" — to mark which kit types to include in the SC analysis.
4
Explore ⚖️ Comparison & 🚛 Fwd Logistics
See scenario cards, cost/kit breakdown, WH vs no-WH savings, and kit-level drill-down.
5
Export your simulation
Go to 📤 Output to download a JSON file capturing every assumption. Share it with colleagues or load it back to recreate the exact simulation.

📊 Upload New Event Dataset (Excel)
Upload a fresh .xlsx file matching the Goodera event data schema (same columns as the original template). The model will rebuild all kit aggregations, city distributions, and filter options from the new data — all your current assumptions (freight rates, WH locations, procurement tiers) are preserved.
Expected sheet structure:
Sheet1 — Event ID, Client, Kit type, Event Location, Kits, Kit Revenue, Procurement Source, Procurement cost, Forward / Reverse logistics cost
Sheet2 — Id, E S T Date
📂 Load Model State
Load a previously exported model state (JSON) to restore assumptions, kit selections, warehouse locations, and scenario settings exactly.

Sc2 vs BAU
Primary objective: reduce total programme cost by — through bulk procurement + sea freight shift + US WH consolidation. Select SC kits in the Forecast tab to populate.
📐 The Four Scenarios
Sc0 — BAU
Historical procurement rates. Direct vendor→event delivery. No warehousing. Baseline.
Sc1 — Bulk Proc
Same logistics as BAU but applies volume discounts on procurement. Lower proc cost, same fwd cost.
Sc2 — 1 Warehouse
Consolidate through WH1. Inbound vendor→WH + WH ops + outbound WH→events. Best for centralised kits.
Sc3 — 2 Warehouses
Each event city is assigned to its nearest WH (WH1 or WH2). Better coverage, shorter final-mile.

📐 Scenario Definitions
Toggle scenarios to include in cost comparisons. All include reverse logistics at historical rates.
Sc0 / BAU — Business as Usual
What is followed today. No bulk procurement advantage. Vendor ships directly to each event.
🏭 Procure from US/India vendor
At historical per-kit rates
→
🚛 Vendor → Event Destination
Direct shipment, historical fwd/kit rate
Procurement: Historical rates (no discount)
Fwd Logistics: Historical fwd/kit × forecast volume
Warehousing: None
Sc1 — Bulk Procurement, Direct Delivery
Bulk procurement for volume discounts, but logistics unchanged (vendor still ships direct to events).
🏭 Bulk procure US/India
At discounted proc rate
→
🚛 Vendor → Event Destination
Direct, historical fwd/kit rate
Procurement: New discounted rates (from T3)
Fwd Logistics: Historical fwd/kit × forecast (same as BAU)
Warehousing: None
Sc2 — 1 Central Warehouse
Bulk procure → route all inventory through one central warehouse → distribute to events.
🏭 Bulk procure
Discounted rate
→
🚛 Inbound to WH1
US: FTL/LTL road
India: Sea/Air + drayage port→WH
→
🏭 WH1 ops
Recv + Proc + Storage
→
🚛 Outbound WH→Event
FTL/LTL per city
Procurement: Discounted rates
Fwd Logistics: Vendor→WH1 inbound + WH ops + WH1→Events outbound
Warehousing: Full WH1 operating costs
Sc3 — 2 Regional Warehouses
Split delivery across two warehouses — each event goes to its nearest warehouse to minimise outbound distance.
🏭 Bulk procure
Discounted
→
🚛 Split to WH1 + WH2
Each city → nearest WH
Same inbound cost model
→
🏭 WH1 + WH2 ops
Pro-rated by volume split
→
🚛 Nearest WH → Event
Shorter routes vs Sc2
Procurement: Discounted rates
Fwd Logistics: Vendor→nearest WH (split) + dual WH ops + WH→Events
Warehousing: WH1 + WH2 costs, pro-rated by city assignment
✨ Best Scenario
For each kit type, the Best scenario is whichever of Sc1/Sc2/Sc3 gives the lowest total cost per kit (procurement + fwd logistics + WH ops + rev logistics).

The Best aggregate sums each kit's cost under its own best scenario, giving the theoretical minimum if you could choose independently per kit.
🎯 Selected Scenario
In the Cost Comparison tab, each kit has a dropdown to pick BAU / Sc1 / Sc2 / Sc3. The default is the Best scenario.

The Selected aggregate reflects the actual decisions you make per kit, giving a realistic implementable cost plan.

Reverse logistics applies at historical rates equally across all scenarios.

📋 Supply Chain Project Charter
Live from model · Objectives · Workstream potential · Roadmap · Governance · Team
🎯 Primary Objective
Build a lean, consolidated supply chain that reduces total programme cost by — through bulk procurement, sea freight consolidation, and centralised US warehousing — enabling more events within the same budget while improving lead-time predictability.
📌 Secondary Objective 1
Establish a demand-driven procurement cycle — replace reactive, event-by-event ordering with a 12-week rolling forecast, reducing emergency air freight below 15% of India volume.
📌 Secondary Objective 2
Build institutional capability — vendor scorecards, SOPs, ERP, and a trained team that executes without single-point-of-failure dependency.
📌 Secondary Objective 3
Implement governance and audit mechanisms giving the Ops Head real-time visibility into cost-per-kit, vendor compliance, and inventory health.
💰 Saving Potential — 3-Year View (selected SC kits · live from model)
Y2 growth: %
Y3 growth: %
How this is calculated: Current = selected kits × selected months saving (from model). Year 1 = current saving prorated to full 12 months based on which months are active. Year 2 = Year 1 × (1 + YoY%). Year 3 = Year 2 × (1 + YoY%). All downstream calculations use the BAU→Sc2 delta as the saving proxy.
Current Period
Selected months · SC kits
—
Saving vs BAU (selected period)
Year 1 (annualised)
Full year at current run rate
—
Prorated to 12 months
Year 2
Year 1 × (1 + growth)
—
—
Year 3
Year 2 × (1 + growth)
—

Select SC kits in Forecast tab to populate
🗓 Workstream Execution Roadmap
🛡 Core Governance Principles
●
3-Quote rule — No PO >$2K without 3 quotes. Logged in ERP.
●
Air freight gate — Air shipment needs Ops Head approval. Target <20% India volume.
●
Ops Head approvals — PO >$15K, new vendor, WH change, budget overrun >10%. 24–48hr SLA.
●
3-way PO match — Finance pays only when PO = GRN = invoice. No manual overrides.
●
Approved vendor list — Procurement from AVL only. New vendor = quality audit + sign-off.
●
Safety stock floors — 4-week minimum for top 20 kits. Below 2 weeks = same-day alert.
📅 Steady-State Cadence
Cadence Activity Owner
DailyShipment tracking, inbound/outbound logLog. Coord
WeeklyOps digest · Safety stock reviewOps Head
Bi-weeklyDemand forecast vs actualDemand Planner
MonthlyCost/kit review · Inventory recon · WH spend · Carrier OTDAll
QuarterlyVendor renegotiation · Price benchmark · Model refreshProc Mgr / Ops Head
AnnualExternal audit · Full inventory count · Strategy reviewExternal / Leadership
👥 Team Required & RACI
Team Roles
Click any cell to edit
Role FTE Key Responsibilities Hire When
🧭 Ops Head1.0Strategy, approvals, vendor relationships, budget, leadership reporting, quarterly model refreshDay 0
🛒 Procurement Manager1.0Vendor negotiation, PO management, AVL, 3-quote compliance, vendor scorecardsMonth 1
🚛 Logistics Coordinator1.0Freight booking (sea + domestic), customs docs, carrier management, freight invoice auditMonth 1
📈 Demand Planner0.5→1.012-week rolling forecast, CRM signal, forecast accuracy tracking, safety stock calculationsMonth 2
🏭 WH Coordinator (US)0.53PL SLA management, inbound/outbound scheduling, inventory reconciliation, WH cost trackingMonth 5
💻 Ops Analyst / ERP Admin0.5ERP admin, data quality, cost reporting, model updates, weekly ops digestMonth 6
RACI Matrix
Activity Ops Head Proc Mgr Log Coord Dmnd Plnr WH Coord
Vendor renegotiationARCII
PO issuance (<$15K)IR/ACCI
Air freight (emergency)ACRII
12-week demand forecastICIR/AI
WH inventory reconciliationIIICR/A
Model refresh (quarterly)R/ACCCI
R = Responsible · A = Accountable · C = Consulted · I = Informed

💾 Save Simulation
📖 How to set up Supabase (one-time, ~5 minutes)
Step 1 — Go to supabase.com → New project (free). Note your Project URL and anon/public key from Settings → API.
Step 2 — Open the SQL editor and run this once: 
create table public.simulations (
  id uuid default gen_random_uuid() primary key,
  user_id uuid references auth.users not null,
  user_email text,
  name text not null,
  description text default ''''
  tags text default '',
  json_state jsonb not null,
  created_at timestamptz default now(),
  updated_at timestamptz default now()
);
alter table public.simulations enable row level security;
create policy "Team can read" on public.simulations for select to authenticated using (true);
create policy "Own insert"    on public.simulations for insert to authenticated with check (auth.uid() = user_id);
create policy "Own update"    on public.simulations for update to authenticated using (auth.uid() = user_id);
create policy "Own delete"    on public.simulations for delete to authenticated using (auth.uid() = user_id);
Step 3 — In Supabase → Authentication → Settings: disable "Confirm email" for easy onboarding (or keep it for security). Invite teammates via Authentication → Users → Invite user.
Step 4 — Paste your Project URL and anon key at the top of this tab. Click Connect. Done.

🔑 Anthropic API Key
The 💡 Insights tab calls the Anthropic API. When using this model outside Claude.ai (e.g. hosted on Netlify), you need to provide your own API key.

Your key is stored only in your browser's localStorage — it never leaves your device except to call Anthropic directly.
Get a key at console.anthropic.com →
